audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for cleansing, centurion faith/trust, authority under authority, Peter's mother-in-law, Isaiah healing citation, discipleship cost, storm calming, and Gadarene/Gerasene deliverance scene using careful spirit-language.

verses.restored: When Jesus came down from the mountain, great crowds followed him.
And look, a man with a skin disease came, bowed/prostrated before him, and said, Lord, if you are willing, you are able to cleanse me.
Stretching out his hand, Jesus touched him, saying, I am willing; be cleansed. And immediately his skin disease was cleansed.
Jesus said to him, See that you tell no one, but go show yourself to the priest and offer the gift Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.
When he entered Capernaum, a centurion came to him, pleading with him,
Lord, my servant is lying in the house paralyzed, terribly tormented.
He said to him, I will come and heal him.
But the centurion answered, Lord, I am not worthy that you should come under my roof, but only speak a word and my servant will be healed.
For I also am a man under authority, having soldiers under me; I say to this one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes, and to my servant, Do this, and he does it.
When Jesus heard, he marveled and said to those following, Amen, I say to you, with no one in Israel have I found such great trust.
I say to you that many will come from east and west and recline with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the reign of the heavens.
But the sons of the reign will be cast into the outer darkness; there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.
Jesus said to the centurion, Go; let it happen for you as you trusted. And the servant was healed in that hour.
Jesus came into Peter's house and saw his mother-in-law lying sick with fever.
He touched her hand, the fever left her, and she rose and served him.
When evening came, they brought to him many troubled by unclean spirits, and he cast out the spirits with a word and healed all who were sick,
so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled: He took our weaknesses and carried our diseases.
Seeing a crowd around him, Jesus commanded them to depart to the other side.
A scribe came and said to him, Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.
Jesus said to him, Foxes have holes and birds of the heavens have nests, but the Son of Humanity has nowhere to lay his head.
Another of the disciples said to him, Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father.
But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
When he entered the boat, his disciples followed him.
And look, a great shaking arose on the sea, so that the boat was being covered by the waves; but he was sleeping.
They came and woke him, saying, Lord, save us; we are perishing.
He said to them, Why are you fearful, ones of little trust? Then he rose, rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was a great calm.
The people marveled, saying, What sort of one is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him?
When he came to the other side, to the region of the Gadarenes, two men troubled by unclean spirits met him, coming out from the tombs, very fierce, so that no one was strong enough to pass that way.
And look, they cried out, What to us and to you, Son of the Divine? Have you come here before the appointed time to torment us?
Far from them a herd of many pigs was feeding.
The spirits pleaded with him, saying, If you cast us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
He said to them, Go. They came out and went into the pigs; and look, the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and died in the waters.
Those tending them fled, went away into the city, and reported everything, including what had happened to the men troubled by spirits.
And look, all the city came out to meet Jesus; and when they saw him, they begged him to depart from their region.
